title: Planchonella monticola
taxonomy: Planchonella monticola is classified in the family Sapotaceae. It belongs to the genus Planchonella. This plant is a part of the order Ebenales and the class Magnoliopsida. The class further belongs to the division Magnoliophyta and the kingdom Plantae. At the most basic taxonomic level, it is classified as a species within the kingdom.

intro: Planchonella monticola, also known as the mountain plum, is a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae, native to the tropical regions of Australia and New Guinea. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various ailments such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Planchonella monticola include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
